commit.message:
8225186

Hi all,

  please review this change that fixes a deadlock of the compiler threads trying to install code. In this case, the Java program holds WhiteBox gc control, waiting for g1RunConcurrentGC() to finish compilation (to be executed). However during compilation the compiler requests a concurrent code cache GC, which blocks because of WhiteBox control never being released.

The suggested fix suppresses the code cache GC (it's a suggestion anyway), making this situation impossible. This is acceptable also because

    WhiteBox is a debug facility, that is not available in typical product runs
    in this test WhiteBox is is more important to control the concurrent cycle compared to having timely (optional) code cache unloading

Testing: gha, test case

Thanks,
  Thomas
